title: Hieracium stenolomum
intro: Hieracium stenolomum, also known as small hawkweed, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its small yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium stenolomum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as stenolomin, and flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium stenolomum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Hieracium stenolomum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Hieracium, which comprises various species commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is a part of the tribe Senecioneae, which is a group of dandelion-like plants. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. These classifications help to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
